app.name: Apollo.io
app.bestFor: All-in-one prospecting: 275M+ contact DB + AI email sequencing. SMB / early-stage default.
app.rankBasis: The only app present in all three source roundups here, with a large independent review footprint and broad SMB sales-team adoption; that combination supports its #1 all-in-one position.
app.pricingFree: Free Starter plan — limited database access and Gmail-only email connection; no card required
app.pricingPaid: Basic $49/user/mo — contact access, CRM integrations and email automation
pros: large database plus outreach in one product
fast onboarding for small sales teams
strong integrations and workflow coverage
cons: credit rules and add-ons can be difficult to predict
users report uneven data accuracy by market
advanced automation and non-Gmail connections require paid tiers

app.name: Clay
app.bestFor: Programmable waterfall enrichment + custom plays. For GTM engineers who want to build.
app.rankBasis: A recurring top recommendation in 2026 AI-SDR comparisons and the category's best-known programmable enrichment workspace, with especially strong GTM-engineering community mindshare.
app.pricingFree: Free plan — 100 data credits/mo and 500 actions/mo
pros: exceptionally flexible enrichment and routing
large provider and integration ecosystem
lets technical GTM teams build highly custom plays
cons: separate data-credit and action meters add complexity
learning curve is steep for simple list building
cost can rise quickly with large waterfalls and AI research

app.name: Instantly
app.bestFor: Cold email at scale, built for deliverability (inbox rotation, send-rate control).
app.rankBasis: Repeatedly included in lead-generation and cold-email shortlists and widely discussed by outbound operators for high-volume sending and deliverability controls.
app.pricingPaid: Growth $47/mo — unlimited email accounts, 1,000 uploaded contacts and 5,000 emails/mo
pros: strong value for teams operating many inboxes
simple campaign setup and scaling controls
deliverability tooling is central rather than an add-on
cons: no standing free plan beyond trial access
lead data and outreach modules use separate pricing
high-volume use still requires careful domain and inbox operations

app.name: 11x
app.bestFor: Fully autonomous outbound (agent 'Alice'). Enterprise, outbound-heavy teams that want volume.
app.rankBasis: Frequently shortlisted in 2026 AI-SDR comparisons for its autonomous 'Alice' agent, but its independent review volume and pricing transparency remain thinner than Apollo, Clay or Instantly.
pros: can automate a large share of repetitive SDR work
single agent-style workflow from research to outreach
built for enterprise outbound volume
cons: no public self-serve pricing
community reports cite long commitments and high total cost
teams have less granular control than with a composable stack

app.name: Artisan
app.bestFor: Autonomous AI SDR (agent 'Ava') that finds, researches, writes and sends. More approachable.
app.rankBasis: A recurring AI-SDR shortlist entry for its 'Ava' agent and broad outbound workflow, though public review depth is still modest compared with established sales platforms.
pros: consolidates research, copy and sending
more guided experience than assembling separate tools
strong focus on autonomous outbound execution
cons: pricing is not published
less flexible than Clay for custom enrichment logic
independent reviewers flag the need for human QA on messaging and targeting

app.name: Cleanlist
app.bestFor: AI waterfall enrichment across 15+ providers, 98% email accuracy, no per-seat fees. Data quality.
app.rankBasis: A newer entrant with limited independent review volume; it ranks here on a tested comparison, transparent pricing and a focused 15-plus-provider enrichment workflow rather than broad market adoption.
app.pricingFree: Free plan — 30 credits/mo (30 email enrichments or about 3 phone enrichments)
app.pricingPaid: Starter $79/mo — 1,500 credits/mo; unused paid credits roll over for up to 3 months
features: AI research agents and scheduled workflows
pros: transparent credit accounting with no per-seat fee
full feature set is available on the free plan
combines enrichment and verification in one workflow
cons: much smaller review base than established vendors
phone enrichment consumes substantially more credits
primarily an enrichment layer rather than a full outreach suite

skill.name: Lead Research Assistant (ComposioHQ)
skill.bestFor: Purpose-built Claude Agent Skill that analyzes your product, finds target companies and returns scored leads with outreach strategies — hosted in the ~69.7k★ awesome-claude-skills collection (stars are for the whole collection).
skill.rankBasis: Hosted inside ComposioHQ's 69.8k-star awesome-claude-skills collection, the largest adoption signal in this track, although the stars belong to the collection rather than this individual skill.
pros: purpose-built end-to-end lead-research workflow
plain SKILL.md format is easy to inspect and adapt
benefits from a very visible skill collection
cons: no independent adoption count for this sub-skill
research quality depends on the agent's available web tools
does not provide a contact database or sending infrastructure

skill.name: Marketing Skills (Corey Haines)
skill.bestFor: Very popular, fast-growing marketing skill collection (~41.5k★) — lead-magnets, cold-email and lead-capture tools are a subset of ~45 broader marketing skills.
skill.rankBasis: About 41.5k GitHub stars and 6.5k forks make this one of the most-adopted marketing skill libraries; lead generation is a useful subset rather than the repository's sole focus.
pros: large community and unusually broad marketing coverage
modular skills can be adopted individually
active maintenance and clear written playbooks
cons: not a dedicated prospecting application
requires external data and execution tools
large scope can be more than a focused outbound team needs

skill.name: Google Maps Scraper (omkarcloud)
skill.bestFor: The most-starred genuine OSS lead-gen tool (~2.9k★) — extracts business emails, phones and socials from Google Maps with enrichment and an API.
skill.rankBasis: At about 2.9k GitHub stars and 447 forks, it is the most-adopted standalone open-source lead-list builder in this set and remains actively maintained.
pros: focused route to local-business lead lists
no recurring software fee when self-hosted
richer enrichment than basic Maps exporters
cons: users must operate the scraper and handle blocking risk
data quality varies by listing and geography
large runs can require proxies and careful rate control

skill.name: OpenOutreach (eracle)
skill.bestFor: Self-hosted agentic, email-first B2B lead-gen agent (~2.5k★) — describe your product and market, and it discovers, qualifies and emails leads.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 2.5k GitHub stars and 474 forks give it the second-strongest standalone OSS adoption signal here; active development reinforces its position despite a sizable issue backlog.
pros: covers discovery through email in one self-hosted project
source is inspectable and customizable
focused specifically on B2B outbound
cons: self-hosting and provider setup are required
email automation creates compliance and deliverability responsibility
you supply external model, search and sending services

skill.name: Goose Skills — GTM & Growth (gooseworks-ai)
skill.bestFor: Library of Growth/GTM Claude & Codex skills (~1.1k★) spanning lead-gen, ads, social, content, SEO and data scraping.
skill.rankBasis: About 1.1k GitHub stars and 193 forks show meaningful early adoption for a newer cross-agent GTM library, but lead generation is only one part of its broader scope.
features: skills for ads, social, content and SEO
pros: works across Claude Code, Codex and Cursor
broad GTM workflow coverage
active project with reusable skill modules
cons: external APIs may add cost and setup
quality and depth vary across a broad skill library
smaller community than the leading marketing-skill collection

skill.name: Cold Outbound Skills (GrowthEngineX)
skill.bestFor: The most focused, purpose-built cold-email skill set (~559★) — 28 skills covering ICP targeting, list building, Prospeo/Google Maps scraping, copy and deliverability.
skill.rankBasis: Its roughly 559 GitHub stars and 200 forks are modest but concrete adoption for a highly focused cold-outbound skill pack; it ranks behind broader, more established repositories.
pros: unusually focused on practical outbound work
covers targeting, copy and deliverability
skills are transparent and easy to customize
cons: small independent community and review footprint
several workflows depend on third-party services
agent-generated campaigns still require compliance and quality review

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt to rank by real adoption, not form factor. This is a genuinely thin OSS/skill space, so it holds only 6 recognizable options and does NOT pad with the many sub-100★ personal skills that exist. The purpose-built Lead Research Assistant (in the ~69.7k★ awesome-claude-skills collection) and Corey Haines' ~41.5k★ marketing skills lead; two real OSS lead-gen tools (google-maps-scraper, OpenOutreach) rank as kind:repo above the smaller focused skills. Removed all MCP-server entries (HubSpot/Salesforce/Smartlead MCP). Star counts verified live 2026-07-24.
